---
# Domain Glossary — 0000003-hook-based-enforcement

*Generated by spec-agent. All agents and humans use these terms.*

---

## Terms

| Term | Definition | Aliases | Used In |
|------|-----------|---------|---------|
| Hook | A script or command registered with an agentic tool that fires automatically on a lifecycle event (e.g. before a tool use, when the agent stops). Hooks run outside the LLM and cannot be skipped by instruction compliance. | hook script, hook command | All tracks |
| PreToolUse | A hook event type that fires before the agent executes a tool call. Used to intercept Write/Edit calls and emit `phase_start`. | pre-tool hook | Track A, Track B |
| Stop | A hook event type that fires when the agent finishes its response turn. Used to emit `phase_end`. | stop hook, turn-end hook | Track A |
| UserPromptSubmit | A hook event type that fires when the human submits a prompt. Used to inject scope context before each turn. | prompt-submit hook | Track B |
| emit-phase-start.mjs | The Node.js script that fires on `PreToolUse` to POST a `phase_start` event to the telemetry backend. | phase-start script | Track A |
| emit-phase-end.mjs | The Node.js script that fires on `Stop` to POST a `phase_end` event with `duration_ms` to the telemetry backend. | phase-end script | Track A |
| flag-file guard | A temp-file sentinel (`{tmpdir}/planifest-telemetry/phase-start-{session_id}-{phase}`) that prevents duplicate `phase_start` emissions per session per phase. | sentinel file, dedup guard | Track A, DD-001 |
| check-design.mjs | The `UserPromptSubmit` hook script that reads `plan/current/design.md` and injects the scope section as `additionalContext`. | scope-injection hook | Track B |
| gate-write.mjs | The `PreToolUse` hook script that blocks Write/Edit when `design.md` is absent or the target path is not in the confirmed component paths. | write gate, path gate | Track B |
| common envelope | The standard JSON payload shape `{session_id, cwd, tool_input, event}` passed to all Planifest hook scripts, regardless of which tool is running. Adapters translate tool-native payloads to this shape. | hook envelope | DD-006, Track C |
| adapter | A per-tool `.mjs` script under `hooks/adapters/` that translates a tool's native hook envelope to the Planifest common envelope before delegating to the shared hook scripts. | tool adapter | Track C |
| Tier 1 | Hook enforcement tier for tools with native shell hook support and full Write/Edit interception: claude-code, cursor, windsurf, cline. | native hooks tier | DD-005 |
| Tier 1b | Hook enforcement tier for codex-cli: native shell hooks activated via `features.codex_hooks = true`; Bash-only (no Windows write gate). | codex hooks tier | DD-005 |
| Tier 2 | Hook enforcement tier for opencode: JS/TS plugin shim (`@planifest/opencode-hooks`) running on Bun. Full enforcement via plugin API. | plugin shim tier | DD-005 |
| Tier 3 | Hook enforcement tier for tools with no programmatic hook system (copilot, antigravity, roo-code). Enforcement is instruction-based only; no deterministic gate. | instructions-only tier | DD-005 |
| Px prefix | The convention that every Planifest pipeline agent response begins with `Px:` where x is the phase number (0–7). `PC:` is used by the change-agent (Change Pipeline). | phase indicator | DD-008 |
| phase table | The structured table shown at Phase 0 open listing all 8 phases (P0–P7) with status indicators. | pipeline overview | DD-007 |
| resume detection | The logic the orchestrator uses on re-entry to determine whether a feature is in progress, which phase it is at, and what was skipped — by scanning `plan/current/` artefacts. | re-entry detection | DD-009 |
| .skips file | `plan/current/.skips` — a plain text file recording human-directed phase skips. One line per skip: `{YYYY-MM-DD} P{x} {phase-name}: {reason}`. Written immediately; deleted by ship-agent at Phase 7. | skips log | DD-008 |
| planifest-ship-agent | The new dedicated Phase 7 skill that raises the PR, writes the changelog, and archives `plan/current/`. Separate from change-agent. | ship agent | Track D, DD-012 |
| archive path | The destination for `plan/current/` after a successful Ship: `plan/archive/{feature-id}-{YYYY-MM-DD}/` with a `-{n}` integer suffix on same-day collisions. | plan archive | DD-012 |
| remediation command | The copy-pasteable shell command shown inline by the Phase 0 hooks health check when hooks are not installed for the detected tool. | inline fix command | DD-010 |
| `.planifest-session` | `{cwd}/.claude/.planifest-session` — project-scoped session ID file created by `emit-phase-start.mjs` when `PLANIFEST_SESSION_ID` env var is absent. Contains a generated UUID. Read by all subsequent hook invocations in the same project. Deleted by ship-agent at Phase 7. | session file | DD-001, REQ-003 |
| `.feature-id` | `plan/current/.feature-id` — written by the ship-agent at the start of Phase 7 archiving. Contains the feature ID. Resume detection reads this file to identify stale artefacts from a prior failed ship attempt. Deleted after archive copy is confirmed. | feature marker | DD-012, REQ-019 |
| `"ship"` phase | The telemetry `phase` enum value used by the ship-agent. Requires a coordinated deploy of the structured-telemetry-mcp schema update before the ship-agent merges. | ship phase value | REQ-021 |
| PLANIFEST_TOOL | Environment variable used to explicitly declare the active tool to the orchestrator, bypassing directory-marker detection. Values: `claude-code`, `cursor`, `windsurf`, `cline`, `codex-cli`, `opencode`, `copilot`, `antigravity`, `roo-code`. | tool env var | DD-007 |
| PLANIFEST_TELEMETRY_URL | Environment variable holding the base URL of the structured-telemetry-mcp backend (e.g. `http://localhost:3456`). Hook scripts use `${PLANIFEST_TELEMETRY_URL}/emit`. Absence is the sentinel for skipping emission. | telemetry URL | Track A, REQ-004 |
| structured-telemetry-mcp | The MCP server that accepts `POST /emit` telemetry events. External to this framework repo; requires a coordinated deploy for the `"ship"` enum addition. | telemetry server, MCP backend | Track A, REQ-021 |
